acceptance_targets: [AWARD_DISCOVERY_ARC_DIFFUSION] Pass if, by the end of the revised library section anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:95 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:105, a cold reader can name one new active problem or changed rule created by that scene that clearly inherits the morning discovery and worsens the chapter's live situation before the night-lost sequence. Fail if the section still mainly communicates that the library is vast and impressive before the chapter restarts at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:109.

[CRAFT_MANOR_RELEASE_CONVENIENCE] Pass if a cold reader can answer, from the revised sequence anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:127; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:137; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:143; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:145, why the Manor released Hermione when it did and can point to one observable consequence that persists beyond fright and note-taking. Fail if she still simply arrives outside her room with no discernible trigger or carry-forward cost.

[R-01] Ch 3's library entry passage no longer mentions mezzanine, rolling ladders, two-story shelves, or the old-paper/preservation-charm atmosphere. The reader learns something about the space they did not already know from Ch 2.
[length_chapter_03_min_words] Chapter word count is >= 5500 while preserving heading contract.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, by the end of the revised library section anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:95 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:105, a cold reader can name one new active problem or changed rule created by that scene that clearly inherits the morning discovery and worsens the chapter's live situation before the night-lost sequence. Fail if the section still mainly communicates that the library is vast and impressive before the chapter restarts at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:109.
findings.problem: The chapter contains three strong discovery beats, but they do not fully accumulate into one escalating unit. The Mark examination lands, then the library section largely resets into admiration and cataloguing, and only afterward does the Manor sequence restart the uncanny engine. That makes the chapter read as elegant setup rather than a shortlist-level chapter whose middle movement worsens the first discovery and actively feeds the last one.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rebuild the middle movement around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:71; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:79; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:85; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:95 so the library beat does more than certify wonder. Keep the Flamel find and the shelf shift, but make one of them create an immediate research problem, changed working rule, or Draco-facing consequence that grows directly out of the Mark anomaly established at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:45 and carries pressure into the night sequence beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:109.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a cold reader can answer, from the revised sequence anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:127; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:137; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:143; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:145, why the Manor released Hermione when it did and can point to one observable consequence that persists beyond fright and note-taking. Fail if she still simply arrives outside her room with no discernible trigger or carry-forward cost.
findings.problem: The lost-Manor sequence generates real fear, but the release beat is author-timed rather than text-earned. Hermione is trapped, then deposited outside the correct door with no shown trigger, threshold, or residual cost, and the chapter asks the reader to accept her inference that the house was 'finished.' Because the chapter's major reveal depends on that abrupt release, the Manor's agency reads as demonstration more than consequence-bearing pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the release logic around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:127; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:133; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:137; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:141 so the Manor's decision to let her out becomes legible on the page. Tie the release to a concrete action, threshold, failed or successful spell, elapsed condition, or specific house response; alternatively, leave a physical or procedural residue at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:141 or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:145 that shows what the detour cost or what the house wanted. Keep her fear and the final realization, but earn the exit.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 3's library entry passage no longer mentions mezzanine, rolling ladders, two-story shelves, or the old-paper/preservation-charm atmosphere. The reader learns something about the space they did not already know from Ch 2.
findings.evidence: Ch 2 line 162: 'Two stories of shelves climbed the walls. A mezzanine ran the perimeter, fitted with rolling ladders and iron rails polished by hands long dead… old paper, dust, preservation charms worked so deeply into the bindings that the air itself seemed lacquered'; Ch 2 line 296: 'shelves rising to the ceiling, mezzanine circling overhead, centuries of collection and theft and scholarship'; Ch 3 line 433: 'Two stories of shelved walls rose around a mezzanine gallery. Rolling ladders clung to brass tracks. Reading tables sat in long shafts of autumn light. Old paper and dust held the air, with preservation charms underneath, mineral and faintly metallic'
findings.problem: The library receives its full establishment in Ch 2 (line 162) and a second confirming view during Hermione's guided tour (line 296). Ch 3 line 433 then re-inventories the same space a third time with near-identical elements: two-story shelves, mezzanine, rolling ladders, old paper/dust/preservation-charm atmosphere. A reader arriving at Ch 3 one chapter after Ch 2 already knows all of these details.
findings.rewrite_direction: Cut or radically compress the Ch 3 library description at line 433. Enter the space through what is NEW for Hermione as a working researcher — the chaos of the collection, the specific texts she discovers. The passage beginning at line 435 ('The southeast corner was arbitrary…') already does this well; let it carry the scene entry.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter word count is >= 5500 while preserving heading contract.
findings.rewrite_direction: Add causally necessary scene material that strengthens the chapter engine, increases pressure, and lands the planned state shift without filler.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel is dark, intense, and emotionally devastating. The premise asks for a love story built inside trauma, in a house that may be manipulating its inhabitants, between two people marked by the same war in different ways. The creative risk is not in the darkness — it is in the tenderness. The hardest thing this novel must do is make the quiet moments as fully rendered as the painful ones, and make the desire as specific as the grief. Do not retreat from the body: the Mark examinations are physical events with physical consequences. The kiss is a physical event. The scar is a physical object on a real arm. Do not retreat from the doubt: the Manor's possible manipulation is not a problem to be solved in act three but a condition to be lived inside for the entire novel. Do not retreat from the ugliness of the drawing room scene or the specificity of what Bellatrix did. And do not retreat from the gentleness of the domestic scenes — tenderness between damaged people is harder to write than pain and should be given equal craft attention.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism is a craft failure equivalent to bad prose. When the scene calls for specificity — in pain, in desire, in shame, in violence, in grief — render it. The drawing room scene must feel like being in the room. The Mark examinations must feel like touch. The scar reveal must feel like exposure. Fading to implication at the moment of consequence wastes every scene that built to that moment.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters except Narcissa. Uncontracted forms reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or Narcissa's default register. An unjustified uncontracted line in casual dialogue should be flagged and revised.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate high-intensity chapters with reflective or domestic ones. Vary scene count: single-scene chapters are allowed for compression; three-scene chapters should not all follow the same internal rhythm. Vary dialogue density — some chapters are conversation-heavy, others are predominantly interior. Never give two consecutive chapters the same structural shape. A chapter that builds to a confrontation should not be followed by another chapter that builds to a confrontation; a quiet aftermath chapter should not be followed by another quiet aftermath chapter. The rhythm of the book as a whole should feel like breathing — expansion and contraction, tension and release, pressure and air.
prose_style_profile.diction: Literary but not ornate. The prose should feel crafted without feeling written — precise where precision matters, rough where roughness is true. Avoid both the polished-prestige-novel voice and the genre-fiction utility voice. The diction should sound like someone telling a story who is very good at telling stories but not performing the act of telling.
forbidden_drift_patterns: The metaphor chain — a comparison that extends across three or more sentences and becomes the scene's organizing image rather than a local effect
Emotional stage directions — 'she felt a surge of' or 'something tightened in his chest' or 'a cold knot formed' as substitutes for specific rendered emotion
The retrospective frame — 'she would later realize' or 'he didn't know then' or other future-looking narrative commentary that drains present-tense urgency
Symmetrical sentence pairs — 'She was X. He was Y.' as a repeated structural pattern, especially in dual-POV transitions
The ambient-magic-as-mood fallback — using the Manor's temperature or the Mark's ache to externalize emotional states that should be rendered through behavior and thought
Catalogue of sensory impressions used as scene-setting rather than scene-building — lists of what a room looks, smells, sounds like that delay rather than create dramatic pressure
The literary-fiction semicolon habit — using semicolons to connect observations that would land harder as separate sentences or would reveal more if left unconnected
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, alternating by chapter. Hermione's chapters are in her interiority — analytical, self-aware, faster. Draco's chapters are in his — more guarded, more sensory, the observations sharper and the self-knowledge harder-won. The POV character's biases should be legible without being announced.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Variable. Default to medium-length sentences with occasional long, accumulating ones and occasional short declaratives for force. Avoid sustained staccato. Avoid sustained flowing prose. The rhythm should shift with scene pressure — tighter under tension, more expansive in quiet and reflective passages.
sensory_bias: tactile — the Mark examinations, the Manor's surfaces, temperature, skin contact, the physical experience of proximity
architectural and spatial — the Manor as sensory environment, corridors, rooms, light, the way space itself communicates
proprioceptive — body awareness, the resonance between them as a felt physical phenomenon, pain and pleasure as data
